preview_text:
The model rates Germany as a modest favorite at 35%, while Ecuador's win probability stands at 33%. The two-point spread reflects a competitive pairing in which neither side commands a decisive edge in the forecast. Ecuador's path to victory remains plausible under the statistical framework applied here.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 32%, only three percentage points behind Germany's win probability. This tight clustering across all three outcomes—no single result exceeds 36%—suggests the match is expected to be closely contested, with genuine uncertainty about the final result rather than dominance by either team.

The market consensus departs notably from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Germany a win probability near 49%, roughly 13–14 percentage points higher than the model's 35%. Conversely, both sources price Ecuador below 27%, compared to the model's 33%. The model and market also diverge on the draw, which the model rates 6–7 points higher. The dashboard presents both estimates without determining which better reflects true match likelihood.
